name: Wolfhart
description: Wolfhart is a prominent hero in Germanic heroic legend, particularly featured in the Nibelungenlied. He is known for his youthful vigor, unparalleled bravery, and unwavering loyalty to his kinsmen, especially Hagen of Tronje. Wolfhart's character is often depicted as rash and eager for battle, driven by a thirst for glory and a fierce sense of honor. He is a key figure in the tragic downfall of the Burgundians at the court of Etzel (Attila the Hun). During the ill-fated visit to Etzel's court, Wolfhart distinguishes himself repeatedly in combat, demonstrating incredible strength and skill in arms. He fearlessly confronts overwhelming odds, engaging in fierce battles against Etzel's warriors. His actions, while valiant, contribute to the escalating violence and ultimately lead to the complete annihilation of the Burgundian heroes. Wolfhart's death in battle is a particularly poignant moment, showcasing his unwavering courage even in the face of certain doom. His character serves as a powerful example of the heroic ideal in Germanic tradition, emphasizing courage, loyalty, and the acceptance of fate, even if it leads to destruction. He represents the tragic flaw inherent in the heroic code – a willingness to fight to the death, even when reason dictates otherwise. Wolfhart's youthful enthusiasm and eagerness for battle make him a compelling and ultimately tragic figure, embodying the destructive consequences of pride and unwavering loyalty.
subtitle: Valiant Burgundian Hero of the Nibelungenlied

keyMyths.title: Arrival at Etzel's Court
keyMyths.description: Wolfhart accompanies the Burgundian kings to Etzel's court, unaware of the impending treachery. He displays pride and readiness for battle, foreshadowing the conflict to come.

keyMyths.title: Wolfhart's Rampage
keyMyths.description: During the escalating conflict at Etzel's court, Wolfhart leads a fierce counterattack against the Hunnic warriors. He slays many enemies, demonstrating his unparalleled combat prowess.

keyMyths.title: Wolfhart's Death
keyMyths.description: Despite his valiant efforts, Wolfhart is eventually overwhelmed and killed in battle. His death symbolizes the tragic fate of the Burgundian heroes and the futility of their struggle.

sources.author: Unknown
